steps.title: Identify the radius and isosceles triangle
steps.content: Since $O$ is the center and $OC = 18$, the radius of the circle is 18. Therefore, $OA = OB = 18$ (both radii). Triangle $OAB$ is isosceles with two equal sides meeting at the center.

steps.title: Find the central angle using the base angles
steps.content:
In isosceles triangle $OAB$, the base angles are equal. Since $\angle OAB = 30^{\circ}$, we also have $\angle OBA = 30^{\circ}$. Using the angle sum in a triangle:
$$\angle AOB = 180^{\circ} - 30^{\circ} - 30^{\circ} = 120^{\circ}$$
This central angle is what we need for the arc length formula.

steps.title: Calculate arc length using the central angle
steps.content:
Arc length formula: $\text{arc length} = \frac{\theta}{360^{\circ}} \times 2\pi r$

Substituting $\theta = 120^{\circ}$ and $r = 18$:
$$\text{arc length} = \frac{120^{\circ}}{360^{\circ}} \times 2\pi(18) = \frac{1}{3} \times 36\pi = 12\pi$$
That confirms B is correct.
